reviewerNotes: Seed gold label from the validated staging smoke clip. Keep this separate from teacher outputs and re-check subtype on the next human review pass.
Human-verified made layup seed. Use as the short-range positive anchor for subtype separation.
Mid-range positive anchor. Keeps the hierarchy from collapsing all made attempts into one generic class.
Catch-and-shoot three seed. Use as the long-range positive anchor.
Missed jumper seed. This is the main negative anchor for miss-vs-made separation.
Validated staging miss clip. Important because the live branch previously flattened misses into made shots.
Blocked-at-the-rim positive anchor. Should stay distinct from steals and generic misses.
Live-ball steal / turnover anchor. Useful for possession-change separation.
Fast-break anchor. Keep separate from made shot clips even when a finish happens at the end.
Hard negative. Dead-ball reset should abstain.
Inbound negative. Ball and hoop are visible, but there is no coherent play.
Dribble-only negative. Should stay below shot_attempt unless a release is visible.
Replay negative. Strong motion but not a live event.
Celebration negative. Useful to prevent post-play scenes from becoming false highlights.
Camera pan negative. Must abstain even if motion is high.
Half-court setup with no event. Helps the classifier abstain on pre-action possession.

rawTeacherOutputs.notes: Teacher is confident this is a made dunk.

reviewerNotes: Runtime stayed on Highlight while teacher suggested a clearer made dunk. Review before promoting to gold.

evidence: ball does not go through hoop

reviewerNotes: Runtime and teacher agree on missed outcome; keep as silver until a human verifies the exact subtype.

rawTeacherOutputs.notes: Teacher points to a turnover / steal.

reviewerNotes: Teacher and runtime both point at turnover, but the clip still needs human review for final gold status.

rawTeacherOutputs.notes: Transition is present, but finish outcome is unclear.

reviewerNotes: Silver transition example. Keep separate from gold until a reviewer confirms whether the sequence contains a finish.
Replay negative. High teacher confidence should still be kept separate until verified as a hard negative.
Dead-ball negative. Should be promoted only after a human verifies the lack of live action.
Celebration negative. Good source for disagreement mining because runtime often over-rewards motion.
Half-court setup without an event. Strong candidate for the abstain bucket.
Missed layup with rebound continuation.
Half-court setup without a shot or turnover.
Rim out on a contested layup.
Camera pan across the crowd / benches.
Celebration after the play is over.
Dead-ball reset with no highlight-worthy action.
Dribble sequence only; no finish, no pass, no turnover.
Inbound pass with no shot attempt.
Replay / scoreboard shot only.
Clear miss that should not be filtered out.
Chasedown block in transition.
Crowd reaction and contact finish.
Short contested jumper, no make.
Offensive rebound into immediate putback.
Tip attempt misses on the second effort.
Deflection leading to a turnover.
